mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in monthly employment levels signals an impending economic contraction, prompting institutional investors to reduce equity exposure and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative employment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP growth and residential housing starts indicates broad-based demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample directional accuracy, or forward returns show no statistical difference between triggered and non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth beyond trend capacity constraints forces firms to raise prices and margins, boosting equity earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns, or the signal yields negative forward returns during high production periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding consumer credit balances reflect rising household liquidity and spending capacity, which directly supports corporate sales and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Consumer credit growth shows zero correlation with subsequent equity returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity valuations remain stable or rise despite high inflation readings, or the inflation threshold fails to predict negative forward returns in a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low unemployment and strong payroll growth indicate a tight labor market that sustains wage-driven consumption, supporting corporate profitability and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict equity direction better than a random baseline, or forward returns show no significant difference between high-tightness and low-tightness regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing permits reflects rising household wealth and construction activity, which stimulates related economic sectors and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts fail to lead equity returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward validation.
